evals.prompt: Create a customer creation form for our Handwerker app. The form should let users add a new Kunde with name, address, phone, and email.
evals.expected_output: A form with large input fields (44px+), German labels above inputs, 16px+ font, big primary button with German verb label, progressive disclosure for optional fields

assertions.name: german-labels-only
assertions.description: All visible UI text must be in German, no English words

assertions.name: touch-targets-48px
assertions.description: All interactive elements must have min 48x48px touch targets

assertions.name: labels-above-inputs
assertions.description: Form labels must appear above input fields, not as placeholders only

assertions.name: font-size-minimum
assertions.description: Body text and labels must be 16px or larger

assertions.name: action-verb-button
assertions.description: Primary button uses a German verb like 'Kunde anlegen' not generic 'OK' or 'Submit'

evals.prompt: Build an invoice status page that shows all invoices with their payment status. Include filters for paid, unpaid, and overdue. Add the ability to mark an invoice as paid.
evals.expected_output: Status page with icon+color+text status indicators, confirmation dialog before marking paid, friendly German empty states, no jargon

assertions.name: status-triple-indicator
assertions.description: Status must use icon + color + text together, never color alone

assertions.name: confirmation-before-paid
assertions.description: Marking invoice as paid must show a confirmation dialog with amount and customer name

assertions.name: no-english-no-jargon
assertions.description: No English words, no technical jargon (Dashboard, Filter, Status -> Übersicht, Anzeigen, Zustand)

assertions.name: empty-state-friendly
assertions.description: Empty state shows a friendly German message with a suggested action, not just 'No data'

assertions.name: confirm-dialog-pattern
assertions.description: Confirmation dialog has Cancel on left (muted), action verb on right (colored), shows what will happen

evals.prompt: Add an error handling screen for when the network connection drops while the user is creating a quote (Angebot). The user should not lose their data.
evals.expected_output: Friendly German error message explaining what happened, no technical jargon, auto-save draft, retry button, back to overview option

assertions.name: plain-german-error
assertions.description: Error message is in plain German, explains what happened AND what to do next

assertions.name: no-technical-details
assertions.description: No stack traces, error codes, 'undefined', 'null', or technical terms shown to user

assertions.name: data-preserved
assertions.description: User input is preserved/auto-saved, message confirms data is not lost

assertions.name: recovery-actions
assertions.description: Provides clear recovery actions: retry button and/or back to overview

assertions.name: no-user-blame
assertions.description: Error message does not blame the user for the failure

evals.prompt: Add a loading state to the 'Angebot erstellen' button. The Angebot creation takes about 2-3 seconds.
evals.expected_output: Button spinner with disabled state, German loading text ('Wird erstellt...'), and a green success confirmation after completion

binary_assertions.name: loading-indicator-shown
binary_assertions.description: Spinner or visual indicator appears on the button after click (operation is 2-3s, which is >300ms threshold)

binary_assertions.name: button-disabled-during-load
binary_assertions.description: Button is disabled during loading to prevent double-submission

binary_assertions.name: german-loading-text
binary_assertions.description: Loading text is in German (e.g. 'Wird erstellt...' or 'Bitte warten...'), not English ('Loading...')

binary_assertions.name: success-confirmation
binary_assertions.description: After success, brief green confirmation shown ('Erstellt ✓' or similar) that fades automatically

binary_assertions.name: no-blocking-overlay
binary_assertions.description: For a button-level operation, uses button spinner — NOT a full-screen blocking overlay (that's only for 2s+ full-page ops)

evals.prompt: Design the mobile navigation for the app. Herr Müller uses it on his phone at the job site.
evals.expected_output: Bottom tab bar, thumb-reachable, all core actions in ≤3 clicks, no hamburger menu, German labels

binary_assertions.name: bottom-tab-bar
binary_assertions.description: Navigation uses a bottom tab bar on mobile, not a hamburger menu or top navigation

binary_assertions.name: three-click-max
binary_assertions.description: All core actions (new Kunde, new Angebot, new Rechnung) are reachable in 3 clicks or fewer from home

binary_assertions.name: 390px-design
binary_assertions.description: Layout is designed for 390px viewport width (mobile-first), no horizontal scrolling

binary_assertions.name: german-tab-labels
binary_assertions.description: Tab bar labels are in German ('Kunden', 'Angebote', 'Rechnungen', 'Übersicht') — no English

binary_assertions.name: thumb-reachable
binary_assertions.description: Core navigation is in the bottom half of the screen, reachable with one thumb

mutations_applied: Added Principle 1 addendum: '≤3 clicks from home screen to any core action' as explicit rule
Added Principle 10 (Loading States): 300ms threshold, button spinner vs full overlay, German loading text, success confirmation
Added Principle 11 (Mobile-First): 390px viewport, bottom tab bar, thumb-zone, iOS zoom fix (16px on inputs), camera capture for file uploads, card lists instead of tables on mobile
Added explicit Simple/Pro Mode toggle mechanism in Section 4 (switch UI, confirmation dialog, persistence in Supabase)
Updated Section 12 (Setup): added note harmonizing with customer-onboarding skill — wizard/Fragebogen is Jonas-guided setup, not self-service
Updated Implementation Checklist: added loading states check, mobile 390px check, auto-save check
